steps.title: Find the slope of line k from the graph
steps.content:
Tracing line k on the graph, it passes through approximately (0, −4) and (5, 0). Using the slope formula:
$$\text{slope of } k = \frac{0 - (-4)}{5 - 0} = \frac{4}{5}$$

steps.title: Apply the perpendicularity rule
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals of each other. If line k has slope $\frac{4}{5}$, then line n has slope:
$$\text{slope of } n = -\frac{1}{\frac{4}{5}} = -\frac{5}{4}$$
This eliminates choices C and D (which have positive slopes).

steps.title: Use the point (4, 7) to find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Line n passes through (4, 7) with slope $-\frac{5}{4}$. Using point-slope form:
$$y - 7 = -\frac{5}{4}(x - 4)$$
$$y - 7 = -\frac{5}{4}x + 5$$
$$y = -\frac{5}{4}x + 12$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
